)", "completion": "[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Skelton-in-Cleveland is a small town located in the civil parish of Skelton and Brotton in North Yorkshire, England. It is noted for its historical significance, having been mentioned in the Domesday Book, which recorded various aspects of life and land ownership in England during the Norman Conquest. Additionally, Skelton is home to a 12th-century castle, which adds to its historical importance.

[[ ## answer ## ]]
The present-day English county of Skelton-in-Cleveland is North Yorkshire.
